About

Marketing as a system, not a list of tasks.

I graduated in International Business with a Marketing specialisation from Hogeschool Rotterdam and stepped into the marketing function at a Dutch B2B SaaS startup shortly after. As the sole marketer in a five-person team, I cover the breadth of the role - strategy, content, SEO, CRM, lead generation, customer onboarding, and translating feedback back to the product team. I like that breadth, and I'm deliberate about prioritising where it has the most leverage.

Before that, I did a marketing internship at Directify, where I worked across an event marketing campaign for Webwinkel Vakdagen - from the strategy and creative side through to the post-event aftermovie.

I use LLMs daily to streamline content workflows, SEO operations, and reporting, and I've built integrations between AI and internal tools. The more interesting question for me is where AI actually moves the needle for marketing teams - and where it just adds noise.

I'm looking for a Marketing Manager, Growth, Content or SEO role in B2B SaaS or tech, where I can keep building on the foundation I've established so far.

The system: positioning → content programme → SEO → CRM → email → reporting loop. Each layer feeds the next instead of running in parallel.

Philosophy

"Good marketing understands people first, channels second. Everything else is execution."

What draws me in

"Psychology is the part of marketing most people skip. How people actually make decisions - especially in long B2B cycles - is the part I find genuinely interesting, and the part technology has changed the most."

Interests

Case studies

Work that moved things forward

A look at how I work - what the problem was, what I did, and what came out the other end.

Current role · Loginex

Building the marketing function and the organic growth engine behind it

Loginex · B2B SaaS · Rotterdam · Jul 2025 - Present
6.8×
Traffic yr/yr
8.8%
Organic CTR
8×
Form submissions

Situation

When I joined, the marketing function still needed to be built up properly - content programme, SEO foundations, consistent positioning, and CRM discipline.

Responsibilities

I cover strategy and execution across content, SEO, lead generation, CRM, and email. I'm also involved in client onboarding and in feeding customer signals back into product and marketing decisions.

What I did

  • Ran a full marketing audit and built a quarterly strategy on the findings.
  • Rebuilt the Loginex website end-to-end on Squarespace - information architecture, page templates, copy, and on-page SEO.
  • Set up a Dutch content programme covering blog articles, case studies, and segmented email flows.
  • Used AI tooling to streamline content briefs, meta descriptions, internal linking, and performance reporting.

Results - all organic, no paid media

Over nine months the organic engine scaled monthly traffic 6.8× year on year and lifted inbound form submissions 8×. The primary CTA form is converting at 11.4%, and organic CTR reached 8.8%.

Internship · Directify

Event marketing campaign - Webwinkel Vakdagen

Directify · Marketing internship · Feb-Jun 2025

Situation

The brief was a campaign for Webwinkel Vakdagen, one of the larger Dutch e-commerce events. It needed to drive awareness in the run-up, bring the right people to the stand, and produce assets that kept earning attention afterwards. Preparation ran for roughly one month, starting two weeks after I joined the internship - a compressed timeline that required fast onboarding and tight prioritisation.

My role

I worked across the campaign lifecycle - research and strategy through to the booth, email, social content, and the post-event aftermovie. The internship pushed me to figure things out as I went, which is where I learned how to own something end-to-end under a real deadline.

What I did

  • Built the campaign strategy and ran a phased timeline from awareness through to post-event nurturing.
  • Designed booth banner assets, wrote and sent the pre-event email, and managed the social content calendar.
  • Coordinated booth setup with external partners and captured content on-site.
  • Scripted, cut, and delivered the post-event aftermovie.
  • Rebuilt and redesigned the Directify website on Squarespace - restructured IA, refreshed templates and copy, and tightened on-page SEO.

Results

We aimed for 20 leads and 5 new clients. The campaign ended at 32 leads and 8 converted clients. The efforts compounded better than the initial forecast.

Campaign strategyAudience research Multi-channel content plan Email newsletterBanner design Booth coordinationLinkedIn contentAftermovie
AI & automation

AI-assisted marketing and no-code automation

Context

A lot of marketing work is structured and repeatable - content briefs, meta descriptions, internal linking, performance reporting. I build automations to handle those parts, so the time goes into the work that actually benefits from a human eye.

What this looks like in practice

I work with no-code tools to connect platforms and route data between systems - CRM, forms, email, internal tools - without needing developer time.

⚡
No-code workflow automation

Comfortable building multi-step automations across the marketing stack - connecting tools, routing data, and removing manual hand-offs without writing code.

✦
LLM-driven content systems

Reusable prompt-based systems for content briefs, SEO meta and titles, internal linking plans, and performance summaries - with consistent brand voice and formatting.

⌬
AI for marketing optimization

Using AI to speed up the work behind content briefs, performance analysis, and reporting - and to support strategy and competitive thinking, not replace it.

⊞
CRM & lead operations

Setting up and maintaining the CRM and the pipelines around it, with automations that keep lead data clean and the team in the loop on what's coming through.

Strategic documents

Marketing strategy and advisory work

Loginex · Directify · University projects · 2022-2026

I've built formal marketing strategy documents that required research, audience analysis, and putting together a coherent plan from scratch - across professional and university work.

Annual Marketing Strategy
Full-year strategy covering brand foundation, market and regulatory context, audience segments, positioning, quarterly campaigns, channel approach, KPI framework, and SEO direction.

Market entry strategy

University advisory · 2024
Digital transformation strategy
Digital transformation strategy for a sustainability-focused B2B company - delivering a digital marketing audit, brand positioning framework, and Dutch-market go-to-market plan.
